rollToDoDefendCodes.title: Roll to Defend Codes
rollToDoDefendCodes.subtitle: Check the current Roll to Defend code status, code rewards, and official places to watch for new drops.
rollToDoDefendCodes.intro: Roll to Defend does not list any active public codes on its Roblox game page or Creator Exchange entry right now. The real in-game rewards to use today are extra luck from playing with friends, extra luck from liking and joining the D:/Drive group, and offline income for more rolls.

redeemSteps.title: How to Redeem Codes
steps: Launch Roll to Defend from the official Roblox game page.
Use the in-game code menu when public codes are added.
Enter one active code exactly as shown.
Claim the reward, then remove the code from your retry list if it moves to expired.
redeemSteps.note: Only Roll to Defend codes belong here; codes from similarly named Roblox defense games are not included.

rollToDoDefendBeginnerGuide.title: Roll to Defend Beginner Guide
rollToDoDefendBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Learn the basic loop: roll units, place defenders, clear zombies, buy zones, and turn income into better pulls.
rollToDoDefendBeginnerGuide.intro: Roll to Defend is built around a simple repeatable loop. Roll for units, use those units to fight zombies, spend income on more strength, unlock zones when your defense can handle it, and use offline income to keep progressing between sessions.

steps.title: Roll Your First Units
steps.body: Start by rolling for units. Your first goal is to get defenders on the field so zombies are being cleared while income starts moving.
steps.playerAction: Roll several times, keep the strongest pulls you see, and avoid spending all early income before you have units placed.

steps.title: Place Units Before Chasing Zones
steps.body: Units need to be on the map to fight zombies. A stronger pull sitting unused does nothing, because even Roblox soldiers respect basic logistics.
steps.playerAction: Place your best visible units where they can attack incoming zombies for the longest time.

steps.title: Let Units Fight Zombies
steps.body: Zombies are the pressure check. If they are leaking through, spend income on more rolls or better placement before pushing into a new zone.
steps.playerAction: Watch the path, identify weak spots, and reinforce the area where zombies survive the longest.

steps.title: Turn Income Into More Rolls
steps.body: Income funds the next set of rolls. The faster you stabilize your defense, the easier it becomes to chase stronger units.
steps.playerAction: After each stable wave cycle, spend part of your income on more unit rolls.

steps.title: Buy Zones When Defense Is Stable
steps.body: Zones are progression, but buying too early can stretch your setup before your units are ready.
steps.playerAction: Unlock a new zone after your current units can handle zombies without constant panic spending.

steps.title: Use Luck Bonuses
steps.body: Roll to Defend gives luck value through social play and the creator group.
steps.playerAction: Play with friends, like the game, and join the D:/Drive group before doing longer roll sessions.

steps.title: Collect Offline Income
steps.body: The game keeps earning income while you are offline, so returning players can immediately fund more rolls or zone progress.
steps.playerAction: When you come back, collect offline income first, then decide whether rolls or zones give the bigger upgrade.

rollToDoDefendTierList.title: Roll to Defend Tier List
rollToDoDefendTierList.subtitle: A practical tier list for deciding which pulls to keep, place first, and build around during zombie waves.
rollToDoDefendTierList.intro: This tier list ranks Roll to Defend pulls by visible rarity value, zombie-clearing usefulness, zone progression value, and public gameplay performance. It focuses on decisions players can act on immediately.

cards.name: Stellar Pirate
cards.bestFor: High-value defense core and long-term keep
cards.why: A public gameplay video highlights Stellar Pirate as a Divine pull with a 1 in 258,300 chance, making it the clearest named high-rarity unit to keep first.

cards.name: Any Divine Pull
cards.bestFor: Replacing lower-rarity units and anchoring stronger waves
cards.why: Divine rarity is the strongest publicly visible rarity category tied to Roll to Defend gameplay, and the game loop rewards stronger rare pulls.

cards.name: Highest-Rarity Current Pull
cards.why: Roll to Defend centers on rolling units and using your strongest pulls to fight zombies, so the highest-rarity unit in your inventory should be placed before filler units.

cards.name: Best Zone-Stabilizing Pull
cards.why: Buying zones is part of the core loop, so the best pull that keeps a new zone stable has high progression value.

cards.name: Starter Damage Pulls
cards.why: Any unit that reliably clears early zombies is worth using until a higher-rarity pull replaces it.

cards.name: Extra Placed Defenders
cards.bestFor: Filling weak spots while income grows
cards.why: Placed units keep zombies under control while you build enough income for more rolls and zones.

cards.name: Outclassed Early Pulls
cards.bestFor: Only filling empty space before better pulls arrive
cards.why: Once you roll stronger units, older low-output pulls should be replaced so your field is not clogged with weak defenders.

rollToDoDefendBestUnits.title: Roll to Defend Best Units
rollToDoDefendBestUnits.subtitle: The safest units and pulls to keep are rare, high-impact defenders that improve zombie clearing and help you push zones without wasting income.
rollToDoDefendBestUnits.intro: Roll to Defend is a roll-based defense game, so the best unit choice is tied to rarity, field impact, and how well a pull helps you clear zombies while income grows. These cards focus on public gameplay and game-visible information.

units.name: Stellar Pirate
units.use: Rare carry pull and long-term keep
units.details: Keep Stellar Pirate over normal pulls and build your defense around it when available. It is the clearest named high-rarity unit shown in public Roll to Defend gameplay.

units.name: Any Divine Pull
units.details: A Divine pull should be kept immediately and placed ahead of lower-rarity filler, because the game rewards rolling stronger units and using them to clear zombie waves.

units.name: Highest-Rarity Current Pull
units.details: Use the strongest rarity visible in your current inventory first. This is the cleanest early rule before exact damage tables are published in-game.

units.name: Zone-Stabilizing Pull
units.details: After buying a zone, keep the pull that best prevents zombie leaks in that area. A unit that stabilizes a new zone can be more valuable than a flashy unused pull.

units.name: Early Path-Coverage Pull
units.use: Fill empty defense space while income grows
units.stage: First session and early income farming
units.details: Use early pulls to cover the zombie path until better rolls arrive. Replace them once higher-rarity units can clear the same section faster.

rollToDoDefendUpgradesGuide.title: Roll to Defend Upgrades: Fix the Bottleneck That Stopped Your Last Run
rollToDoDefendUpgradesGuide.subtitle: Roll to Defend is built around rolling units, fighting zombie waves, buying zones, using friends luck, and reinvesting offline income. Spend income on the next clear bottleneck instead of spreading it across every option.
rollToDoDefendUpgradesGuide.intro: Use upgrades as a survival tool first and an economy tool second. If zombies are reaching the base, strengthen damage or coverage before buying a new zone. If the defense is stable, then put income into better roll sessions, zone progression, and longer-term income growth.

items.buy_when: After rolling and placing units, check the in-game upgrade options before spending all income on more rolls.
items.best_use: Use the upgrade controls inside the Roblox experience and keep purchases tied to the current run goal.
items.delay_when: You have not identified whether the current problem is damage, coverage, roll quality, or zone timing.

items.buy_when: Zombies survive long enough to reach your defense or force last-second spending.
items.best_use: Strengthen the unit or setup that already clears the most zombies.
items.delay_when: Enemies are leaking because of placement gaps rather than low kill speed.

items.buy_when: Groups slip past even though your main damage unit is performing well.
items.best_use: Improve the way your defense covers the zombie path before chasing another random roll.
items.delay_when: Your current units cannot kill basic waves quickly enough.

items.buy_when: You have saved income and your friends luck or group extra luck setup is active.
items.best_use: Roll in a prepared session so more income goes into useful unit pulls.
items.delay_when: Your current wave is already failing and needs direct defense upgrades first.

items.buy_when: A duplicate gives a visible improvement over the unit already placed.
items.best_use: Keep the stronger copy in the active defense and use extras only when they create clear upgrade value.
items.delay_when: Replacing a working unit would reduce coverage or leave a lane open.

items.buy_when: Current waves clear cleanly and income is building faster than your defense needs it.
items.best_use: Prepare for the next zone by building one reliable damage source and one stable coverage layer.
items.delay_when: A new zone would make the next zombie pressure harder before your defense is ready.

items.buy_when: You return to the game with offline earnings available.
items.best_use: Spend the returned income on the weakness that ended your last run, then start rolling or pushing zones.
items.delay_when: You have not checked whether damage, coverage, roll quality, or zone timing is the real bottleneck.

rollToDoDefendZonesGuide.title: Roll to Defend Zones: Buy After Your Defense Is Stable
rollToDoDefendZonesGuide.subtitle: Zones are part of the main Roll to Defend loop: roll units, fight zombies, buy zones, and unlock new areas. The best time to expand is after your current setup can clear waves without emergency spending.
rollToDoDefendZonesGuide.intro: Treat every zone purchase as a progression checkpoint. A new zone should help your income or placement value, but it can also expose weak damage, poor coverage, or a rushed roll setup. Clear the current pressure first, then expand.

steps.title: Stabilize the current zone
steps.action: Roll and place units until the current zombie waves clear without panic spending.
steps.whyItMatters: Stable clears give you time to save income instead of constantly patching leaks.
steps.readySignal: Zombies are cleared before they threaten the base.

steps.title: Check your weakest point
steps.action: Decide whether your last problem was damage, coverage, unit quality, or income speed.
steps.whyItMatters: Buying a zone before fixing the real weakness makes the next area harder to control.
steps.readySignal: You can name the bottleneck and have already improved it.

steps.title: Buy the next zone with backup income
steps.action: Purchase a new zone after your defense is stable and you still have enough income to react after the unlock.
steps.whyItMatters: Zones push progression, but the first waves after expanding can expose weak spending choices.
steps.readySignal: You are not spending your entire balance on the zone alone.

steps.title: Rebuild placement around the new area
steps.action: Recheck where zombies travel and focus on the lane or area that now creates the most pressure.
steps.whyItMatters: New zones can change which units feel valuable, especially when coverage becomes more important than raw rolling.
steps.readySignal: Your best damage source still has useful coverage after the zone unlock.

steps.title: Feed the higher progression loop
steps.action: Put the improved income flow back into better rolls, stronger units, and the next zone goal.
steps.whyItMatters: Zones are not the end of progression; they feed the roll-and-defend cycle.
steps.readySignal: Your income supports upgrades and rolls without breaking the defense.

steps.title: Pause expansion after a rough unlock
steps.action: If the new zone makes waves feel worse, stop buying zones and fix the failing defense layer first.
steps.whyItMatters: Pausing expansion keeps income available for the damage or coverage problem that appeared after the unlock.
steps.readySignal: The new zone clears consistently before you plan the next purchase.

rollToDoDefendLuckAndOfflineIncome.title: Roll to Defend Luck & Offline Income: Stack Luck, Spend Income Smartly
rollToDoDefendLuckAndOfflineIncome.subtitle: Roll to Defend highlights friends luck, group extra luck, and offline income directly in its game description. Put those systems in order before a long roll session.
rollToDoDefendLuckAndOfflineIncome.intro: Luck setup belongs before the rolling spree. When you return with offline income, collect it, identify the weakest part of your last run, and reinvest before buying a new zone.

sections.heading: Friends luck
sections.summary: Play with friends when you are preparing to spend a saved income stack on rolls.
sections.content: The game description calls out playing with friends for more luck. Use that bonus during planned roll sessions instead of spending every small income pickup immediately.
checklist: Join the same server as friends before rolling heavily.
Save income for a focused roll session when possible.
Roll after the luck setup is active.

sections.heading: Group extra luck
sections.summary: Like the experience and join the creator group for the extra luck path shown by the game.
sections.content: The Roblox page says to like the game and join the group for extra luck. Treat this as a basic setup step before judging whether your rolls feel strong.
checklist: Open the Roll to Defend Roblox page.
Join the D:/Drive Roblox community connected to the game.

sections.heading: Roll session preparation
sections.summary: Prepare luck first, then roll with a clear goal.
sections.content: A useful roll session starts with a stable defense, active luck sources, and a clear target: better damage, better coverage, or a replacement for a weak unit.
checklist: Activate friends luck and group extra luck setup.
Spend rolls toward the weakness that matters most.

sections.heading: Offline income return
sections.summary: Collect offline income at the start of the session and turn it into immediate progress.
sections.content: Roll to Defend states that you continue to earn income offline. When you return, use that income to fix the last bottleneck before rolling heavily or buying a new zone.
checklist: Upgrade damage or coverage if the last wave leaked.
Buy zones only after the defense is stable.

sections.heading: Best income order after returning
sections.summary: Fix survival first, roll second, expand third.
sections.content: Offline income is most useful when it keeps the next run from failing for the same reason. Spend on survival if zombies are breaking through, then roll or zone-push once the defense is steady.
checklist: Damage problem: strengthen the main clear unit.
Coverage problem: improve placement or support units.
Stable defense: roll with luck or prepare the next zone.

rollToDoDefendOfficialLinks.title: Roll to Defend Official Links: Start from the Roblox Game Page
rollToDoDefendOfficialLinks.subtitle: The safest Roll to Defend entry points are the Roblox experience page and the D:/Drive creator community. Use Roblox-linked routes before trusting external invites, stores, or boards.
rollToDoDefendOfficialLinks.intro: Start from Roblox before using any community link. The game page provides the experience, store, and server entry points, while the creator community is the group route tied to extra luck.

links.title: Roblox experience page
links.useFor: Play Roll to Defend, read the current description, favorite the game, and reach the Store or Servers tabs.

links.title: D:/Drive creator community
links.status: Linked from the Roblox experience creator
links.useFor: Join the creator group route connected to the extra luck prompt.

links.title: Store tab
links.status: Available from the Roblox game page
links.useFor: Check game products from the official Roblox page instead of third-party purchase claims.

links.title: Servers tab
links.status: Available from the Roblox game page
links.useFor: Join available public servers or choose a server from the Roblox interface.

links.title: Creator Exchange listing
links.status: Lists D:/Drive as owner and Roll to Defend as the community label
links.useFor: Check public listing details such as owner, genre, code status, and the listed community label.

links.title: Discord entry
links.status: Use only from a Roblox, in-game, or creator-linked source
links.useFor: Avoid invite pages that ask for Roblox passwords, scripts, free Robux steps, or unrelated redirects.

links.title: Trello board
links.status: No Trello link is shown on the visible Roblox game page
links.useFor: Use the Roblox page as the source of record until a Trello is posted from an official creator route.

links.title: YouTube walkthroughs
links.useFor: Use videos for visual walkthroughs, but confirm links and purchases through Roblox or the creator group.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Roll to Defend on Roblox.

questions.question: What is Roll to Defend on Roblox?
questions.answer: Roll to Defend is a Roblox roll-based zombie defense simulator. You roll units, place defenders to fight zombie waves, buy zones, stack luck with friends and the D:/Drive group, and reinvest offline income to build stronger runs.

questions.question: Are there active Roll to Defend codes right now?
questions.answer: Roll to Defend does not show active public codes on its Roblox game page right now. The current in-game rewards are friend luck, group luck from liking and joining D:/Drive, and offline income that funds more rolls.

questions.question: How does luck work in Roll to Defend?
questions.answer: Roll to Defend gives luck through social play and the creator group. Play with friends for friend luck, and like the game plus join the D:/Drive group for extra luck before longer roll sessions.

questions.question: What are zones in Roll to Defend?
questions.answer: Zones in Roll to Defend are progression areas you buy with income. The safest time to unlock a new zone is after your current defense can clear zombie waves without panic spending.

questions.question: Does Roll to Defend have offline income?
questions.answer: Yes. Roll to Defend keeps earning income while you are offline. When you return, collect the offline income first and spend it on the weakness that ended your last run before buying a new zone.

questions.question: Where can I play Roll to Defend?
questions.answer: You can play Roll to Defend on the official Roblox game page. The D:/Drive creator community is the group route connected to the extra luck prompt.
